title: glechoma pusilla
intro: Glechoma pusilla, also known as Creeping Speedwell or Creeping Jenny, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, blue or white flowers and creeping stems. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Glechoma pusilla include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote respiratory health. In herbal preparations, it can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
